texto: The word bisexual had stood out so bright and clear in my head that all else had ceased to exist. Bisexual. I had a word. I understood; it was me… a nice clear label that said it all. I didn't have to choose. I didn’t have to be not attracted to either guys or girls — a prospect I had found utterly absurd and likely impossible, but had thought was perhaps necessary. Now it wasn’t necessary. Now it was okay to be me. I was not unheard of. Bisexual.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Harrie Farrow sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “The word bisexual had stood out so bright and clear in my head that all else had ceased to exist.…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, bisexual, lgbt, útil em momentos de lgbt e pride.
